name: Soufiane Tayebi
title: GPU Architecture Engineer
summary: MEng candidate specializing in GPU architecture and parallel computing, with hands-on experience developing optimized solutions for HPC systems.

description: Engineered AI4S applications on NVIDIA Jetson Nano using Python and C++ within Linux development environments.
Optimized deep learning models with CUDA programming and TensorRT, achieving 10+ FPS gain for real-time inference.
Implemented multi-modality AI systems integrating speech-to-text, LLMs, and text-to-speech with voice cloning.
Developed accelerated computing pipelines for autonomous driving including lane tracing and obstacle avoidance.
Benchmarked model inference performance across real-world environments, ensuring reliable deployment.
Collaborated on full-stack development of control panels for live streaming and system monitoring via sockets.

projects.name: GPU Driver Profiling and Tracing on Linux
description: Engineered a GPU profiling tool using CUDA C++ and NVIDIA Driver APIs on Linux, accelerating computing performance analysis by 40%.
Optimized AI model inference benchmarking by measuring GPU utilization and kernel execution via thread-safe sampling.
Developed accelerated computing tools with eBPF and CMake, improving real-time workload analysis for AI4Science applications.

projects.name: CUDA-Accelerated Raytracer in OpenGL
description: Engineered CUDA C++ accelerated computing GPU path tracer implementing parallel ray generation achieving 98% GPU utilization.
Optimized CUDA kernels using cuRAND for Monte Carlo image reconstruction boosting rendering performance by 40%.
Implemented OpenGL interop for full-stack GPU rendering pipeline demonstrating strong Linux development environment proficiency.
Developed AI-powered denoising model using PyTorch for real-time rendering reducing noise by 75% in final output.
Collaborated with cross-functional teams to integrate physics-based rendering with modern AI transformer architectures.

projects.name: Vulkan GPU Physics Engine in C++ (Ongoing)
description: Engineered a Vulkan-based AI renderer using Python and C++ for accelerated computing with explicit GPU memory management.
Optimized CPU-GPU synchronization achieving 99% frame pacing efficiency via swap chain management and V-Sync.
Developed graphics pipelines with CUDA-accelerated point lights and raytracing physics for computational modeling.
Implemented AI model inference benchmarks using PyTorch and transformers within Linux development environments.
Collaborated on multi-functional teams to deliver AI4S tools leveraging NVIDIA SDKs for research projects.

certifications: NVIDIA -- AI on NVIDIA Jetson Nano
